reports.title: What Is Artificial Intelligence?
reports.reason: writer: could not produce original article (10 facts, typed=10)

sample: Artificial Intelligence is the capability of computational systems to perform tasks typically associated with human intelligence. It matters because knowing what it is—and is not—changes real decisions. You encounter it in everyday products

This follows Alan Turings proposal in his paper Computing Machinery and Intelligence, in which the question, Can machines think?, is replaced by asking whether machines can convincingly imitate a human in its responses to human-posed questi

Due to its generality, the field is studied in many other disciplines, such as game theory, control theory, operations research, information theory,. For example, game theory, control theory, and operations research.
Artificial Intelligence is clearer when you separate definition, purpose, and use. Start with what it is, then why it exists, then where it shows up in practice.
Once the definition is clear, the next question is why Artificial Intelligence exists at all.
